channel.title: The Grant Desk
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk.

item.title: Musikfonds: Large Project Funding (New and Experimental Music) 2026 (Germany)
item.description:
Organisation: musikfonds e.V.

Location: Germany. Applicants and projects based in Germany; excludes pure audio/video productions, university and amateur-only projects.

Award: Up to EUR 50,000 per project (minimum 10 percent co-financing; artist fee at least EUR 300 per concert). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based.

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

Musikfonds Large Project Funding supports new, experimental and contemporary music projects with grants of up to EUR 50,000. A minimum 10 percent co-financing is required and the artist fee must be at least EUR 300 per concert. Applicants and projects must be based in Germany; pure audio or video productions, university projects and amateur-only projects are excluded. The application window runs 1 to 30 September 2026. NOTE: research-sourced; confirm current figures and window dates on the Musikfonds site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sony Innovation Fund (rolling, equity investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Sony Ventures Corporation (Sony Innovation Fund)

Location: Global; offices in the US (Bay Area, Los Angeles), London/EU, Tokyo, Bangalore and Israel.

Award: Corporate venture capital; typical check roughly USD 100,000 to USD 5 million (reported AUM USD 250 million plus), plus mentorship and access to Sony's network. This is an equity INVESTMENT, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Equity investment; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Sony Innovation Fund (now under Sony Ventures Corporation) is Sony's corporate venture capital arm, investing in early-stage (Seed, Series A and Series B) transformational-technology startups across business, entertainment and society, including entertainment, gaming, AI, robotics, fintech, climate and blockchain. Music, entertainment and AI startups fit within its thesis, though it is not a music-only fund. Typical checks are roughly USD 100,000 to USD 5 million, alongside mentorship and access to Sony's network and R&D. It is an equity investment, not a grant. Global, with offices in the US, London/EU, Tokyo, Bangalore and Israel. Startups engage on a rolling basis via the contact and inquiry form. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: UNIIQ: Proof-of-Concept Fund (rolling, Zuid-Holland)
item.description:
Organisation: UNIIQ

Location: Zuid-Holland (South Holland), Netherlands only. Early-stage research-driven startups.

Award: Around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (investment, not a grant). Total fund EUR 50 million. No application fee.

Duration: Investment via convertible loan; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

UNIIQ is a proof-of-concept investment fund for the earliest, riskiest phase of company building in Zuid-Holland (South Holland). It invests around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (for example redeemed over about three years or converted at a follow-on valuation), drawn from a EUR 50 million fund. It is an investment, not a grant. Eligible companies are early-stage, research-driven startups based in Zuid-Holland, typically in life sciences, deep tech and high tech. Intake is continuous via an orientation meeting followed by a funding application. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROM InWest: Startup and Scale-up Financing (rolling, Noord-Holland)
item.description:
Organisation: ROM InWest (Regional Development Company for Noord-Holland)

Location: Noord-Holland (North Holland), Netherlands. Startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs.

Award: Financing of EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (not a gift); cannot exceed 49.9 percent of a financing round. This is investment, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Investment; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

ROM InWest is the regional development company for Noord-Holland (North Holland). It finances startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs working on food, energy, healthcare and circular-economy transitions, providing EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (explicitly not a gift), and not exceeding 49.9 percent of a financing round. It is an investment, not a grant. Companies must be based in Noord-Holland. Intake is continuous, starting with a quick-scan tool. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Async Museum: Open Call for Web-Based Art 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Async Museum

Location: Online (independent digital space); open to artists worldwide.

Award: Exhibition and visibility opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected web-based artworks are presented in Async Museum's independent online space. No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Rolling open call (exhibition in Async Museum's online space)

Deadline: 01 Dec 2026

Async Museum is an independent digital space dedicated to web art with a distinctive point of view, existing to discover artists who use the web as their primary medium and to recognise their work as a new aesthetic. Its core philosophy is that the web browser is a twenty-first century canvas. Async Museum is officially seeking submissions of web-based artworks, welcoming both established practitioners and emerging voices who are pushing the boundaries of what the next generation of art can be. NOTE: this is an exhibition opportunity for web-based art rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. To share your practice and submit your work, visit https://asyncmuseum.com/submit. Deadline: 1 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Restless Egg: Batch 2, Luxury Technology (Accelerator for Artist-Founders) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Restless Egg

Location: London-based incubator (UK); open to creative "artist-founders" internationally, with in-person gatherings (a first-batch salon was held at Sybil in Berlin).

Award: Equity-based investment (NOT a non-dilutive grant): USD 100,000 initial investment for 5% equity, plus up to USD 175,000 in follow-on investment when predefined performance metrics are met. Includes a high-touch accelerator program and access to a network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors.

Duration: Six-month accelerator program

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Restless Egg is a London-based incubator for "artist-founders", an emerging genre of creative people whose work sits between art, technology and building commercial products. It invests in creative founders and runs a six-month, high-touch accelerator program to help them build commercially successful and scalable companies at the bleeding edge of culture and technology. BATCH 2 (LUXURY TECHNOLOGY) is open for applications, seeking founders building luxury technologies: commercial products that use AI and other emerging technologies to unlock richer, stranger, more meaningful forms of human experience, not just more efficiency. For this batch Restless Egg has doubled its initial investment to USD 100,000 for 5% equity, and increased its follow-on investment to up to USD 175,000, aiming to give founders more runway, more support and more room to build the most ambitious companies. The program supports rapid learning, sharper product and narrative development, and real investability through close engagement with Restless Egg and its network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors; the follow-on is released when predefined performance metrics are met. NOTE: this is a dilutive equity investment (it takes a 5% stake), not a non-dilutive grant or fellowship. Applications are open (no fixed deadline stated). Apply at https://apply.restlessegg.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prix Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize 2026 (OIF / ALECSO)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and Arab League Educational, Cultural and Scientific Organization (ALECSO)

Location: International (Francophone zone and Arab world)

Award: EUR 10,000 (funded equally by OIF and ALECSO). No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize (19th edition, 2026) is awarded jointly by the Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and ALECSO to recognise excellence in literary and humanities/social sciences translation between French and Arabic in either direction. Cash award: EUR 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual translators, universities and higher education institutions, research centres, national associations and unions, and publishing houses from the Arab world and Francophone space. Works must be literary or humanities/social sciences texts translated between French and Arabic (either direction), published within 3 years prior to the application year, and must be a first translation of the original. Self-published or photocopied works excluded. One work maximum per candidate. Applications submitted exclusively electronically; required materials: CV, presentation of translation work, copies of both translated and original texts. Deadline: 30 June 2026. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Siirtymä Ambient Music Festival 2026: Open Call (Tampere, Finland)
item.description:
Organisation: Suomen Ambientyhdistys ry (Finnish Ambient Association)

Location: Tampere, Finland (multiple simultaneous venues, site-specific)

Award: Technical support provided. No artist fee publicly confirmed. No application fee.

Duration: Festival date: 5 September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Siirtymä Ambient Music Festival 2026 is a free, community-driven festival in Tampere, Finland, organised by Suomen Ambientyhdistys ry (Finnish Ambient Association). The festival uses overlapping simultaneous performances across multiple locations, emphasising spatial and ambient experience over traditional stage formats. Open to artists and collectives working in ambient music, sound art, spatial installations, and interdisciplinary practices; no career-stage restriction; the festival prioritises emerging and lesser-known artists. The 2026 theme focuses on strengthening diversity, creating multiple voices, and finding new ways to encounter one another and the urban environment. Technical support is provided to selected participants. No travel or accommodation funding is publicly confirmed. NOTE: deadline of June 30 is from the Community Arts Network PDF; verify at https://siirtyma.com/en/siirtym-2026 before submitting. Contact Suomen Ambientyhdistys ry directly for application details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 - Research Track
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: London, UK; in-person, full-time.

Award: £12,000 salary for the three-month term, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch provided. Visa sponsorship (3-month temporary work visa) available. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 18 January to 9 April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

GovAI's UK Winter Fellowship (Research Track) is a three-month in-person fellowship in London for researchers and policy professionals seeking to enter or advance careers in AI governance and policy. Fellows conduct independent research on a topic of their choice, paired with a GovAI supervisor from the team or network; the first two weeks are spent exploring the AI governance landscape before a research proposal is finalised. Research outputs may include a report, white paper, journal article, op-ed, or blog post targeted at an audience relevant to AI governance. Alongside the project, fellows attend expert Q&As, weekly seminars, skills workshops, work-in-progress meetings, and social events, and are encouraged to discuss follow-on career opportunities with GovAI's team and network. GovAI alumni have gone on to roles in US, UK, and EU government; leading AI companies (Anthropic, DeepMind, OpenAI); top think tanks (CSET, RAND); and leading universities (Oxford, Cambridge). AMOUNT: £12,000 salary for three months, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch. Visa sponsorship for a 3-month temporary work visa is available; successful visa applicants must remain in their country of application while the visa is processed. ELIGIBILITY: open to a wide range of backgrounds (government, academia, industry, civil society); no strict degree requirements; relevant areas include public policy, political science, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management. Senior candidates who need accommodations should email fellowships@governance.ai with subject line 'senior candidate winter fellowship 2027' and a CV. SELECTION CRITERIA: quality and clarity of written work, relevant expertise, judgement in prioritising research directions, and team fit (intellectual honesty, openness to feedback, comfort with uncertainty). APPLICATION PROCESS: (1) written application and automated abstract reasoning assessment; (2) paid remote work test (August 2026); (3) remote interview and reference checks (September 2026); decisions communicated October 2026. Deadline: 12 July 2026, 23:59 BST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Musikfonds: Small Project Funding (New and Experimental Music) 2026 (Germany)
item.description:
Organisation: musikfonds e.V.

Location: Germany. Applicants and projects based in Germany; priority to rural and structurally weaker regions.

Award: Up to EUR 3,000 per project (minimum 10 percent co-financing; artist fee at least EUR 350 per concert). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Musikfonds Small Project Funding supports new, experimental and contemporary music projects with grants of up to EUR 3,000, prioritising projects in rural and structurally weaker regions of Germany. A minimum 10 percent co-financing is required and the artist fee must be at least EUR 350 per concert. Applicants and projects must be based in Germany. Application windows close 31 August 2026 and 30 November 2026. NOTE: research-sourced; confirm current figures and window dates on the Musikfonds site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Amarte Fonds: Development and Project Contributions (rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Amarte Fonds

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). Applicants must be based there.

Award: Development contribution up to EUR 2,750; project contribution up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent paid in advance, remainder after the report is approved. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; rolling decisions.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Amarte Fonds is a private Dutch foundation that supports the arts, with rolling (continuous) contributions for visual artists and independent curators. There are two contribution types: a development contribution of up to EUR 2,750 and a project contribution of up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent is paid in advance, with the remainder after the final report is approved. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). The fund also supports work across theatre, film, music, literature and interdisciplinary projects through specific programmes. There is no fixed deadline and no application fee; decisions are relatively fast. Apply year-round via the online portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Abbey Road Red: Music-Tech Incubator (rolling, London)
item.description:
Organisation: Abbey Road Red (Abbey Road Studios / Universal Music Group)

Location: London, UK. Global applicants; selected startups are based in London during the 6-month programme.

Award: In-kind incubator: access to Abbey Road and UMG studios, experts, facilities and the global label and publisher network, plus mentorship and some finance. Universal Music Group takes a 2 percent equity stake plus a participation right in future financing rounds. This is NOT a grant. No application fee.

Duration: 6-month incubator programme.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Abbey Road Red is the music-technology incubator run by Abbey Road Studios (part of Universal Music Group). It is primarily an in-kind and mentorship programme rather than a cash grant: selected early-stage music-tech startups (building tools to create, capture or connect music) get access to Abbey Road and UMG studios, experts and facilities, and the global label and publisher network, plus mentorship and some finance, over a 6-month programme. In exchange, Universal Music Group takes a 2 percent equity stake plus a participation right in future financing rounds. Open to startups worldwide; participants are based in London during the programme. Applications are rolling via the open form on the Abbey Road Red site. No application fee. Note: this is an equity-based incubator, not a grant.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Yamaha Music Innovations Fund (rolling, equity investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Yamaha Music Innovations, LLC

Location: Silicon Valley, USA. Global reach; no stated geographic restriction.

Award: USD 50 million venture fund making early-stage equity investments. This is an equity INVESTMENT, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Equity investment; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Yamaha Music Innovations Fund is a USD 50 million venture fund (Yamaha Music Innovations Fund I, LP, managed by Yamaha Music Innovations, LLC) that makes early-stage equity investments in startups across four themes: creative tools and infrastructure; innovative and interactive media; community and collaboration; and empowerment and accessibility (including AI for music and video production, instruments and audio gear, and creator streaming and monetisation). It is an equity investment vehicle, not a grant. Based in Silicon Valley with global reach and no stated geographic restriction. Startups pitch on a rolling basis via the fund's Pitch Us form. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: eidolon Grant 2026 (Vernacular Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: eidolon

Location: International; open worldwide.

Award: A total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed across two application categories. No application fee.

Duration: Annual grant; supported projects contribute to the institution's program in the coming year

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The eidolon Grant is an international programme, presented annually, for projects that explore, promote and conserve vernacular photography. It is open to artists, academics, professionals, researchers, collectors and vernacular photography enthusiasts whose past work and proposed project is centred around the image heritage of everyday photography. The grant aims to identify phenomena, collections, histories, practices and trends within vernacular photography, offering new interpretations and analyses, and thematising both photographic heritages and contemporary photographic practices. Applications can be made under two categories, with a total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed. Each chosen project will contribute to the enrichment of eidolon's program in the coming year. The deadline for submission is 30 September 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Apply via https://everydayphotography.org/centre/grant-2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World (Joint Funding Call)
item.description:
Organisation: Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, the Advanced Research & Invention Agency (ARIA), the Cooperative AI Foundation, and Google.org

Location: Global; open worldwide, with collaborations across geographic boundaries encouraged.

Award: Two funding tiers (applicants may apply to either or both), projects 1 to 2 years. Tier 1: up to USD 300,000 for exploratory research, pilot studies, or focused technical investigations. Tier 2: USD 300,000 to USD 1,000,000 for more ambitious or collaborative projects. For projects funded by Schmidt Sciences, indirect costs must be at or below 10%. No application fee.

Duration: 1 to 2 years

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

A joint philanthropic funding call by Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, ARIA, the Cooperative AI Foundation and Google.org to catalyse the foundational scientific research needed to understand, evaluate and control risks emerging from large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents deployed by multiple actors (multi-principal, multi-agent settings). The call argues that focusing only on the safety and alignment of individual models is insufficient, and seeks system-level approaches. RESEARCH AGENDA (four clusters): (1) Sandboxes and Testbeds, realistic, reproducible multi-agent environments for studying populations of frontier-model agents; (2) The Science of Agent Networks, how collective capabilities emerge and scale, how networks fail, and how dangerous population-level properties can be detected; (3) Strengthening Agent Infrastructure, evaluating and stress-testing primitives such as identity, verifiability, reputation, communication and commitment; (4) Multi-Agent Oversight and Control, detection, attribution, security and intervention methods to keep deployed agent populations safe at scale. Proposals may target one cluster or span several, with priority on depth over breadth and on realistic sandboxes and testbeds. OUT OF SCOPE: single-agent safety, capability advancement without a clear safety motivation, toy systems, naive application of pre-existing solutions, commercial product development, and purely conceptual/policy work. ELIGIBILITY: individual researchers, research teams, research institutions and multi-institution collaborations across universities, national laboratories, institutes and nonprofit research organisations; open globally. Informational webinars on 30 June and 23 July 2026; notification of decisions in autumn 2026. Proposals due 8 August 2026 by 11:59pm Anywhere on Earth. Contact: multiagentsafety@schmidtsciences.org. Apply at https://schmidtsciences.smapply.io/prog/scaling_ai_safety_for_a_multi_agent_world/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Social Shifters Global Innovation Challenge 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Social Shifters

Location: Global; open to youth-led ventures from anywhere in the world.

Award: Multiple awards of up to USD 15,000 each (reported grants from USD 3,000 to USD 15,000), plus pitch coaching, a finalist showcase, free founder support from registration, and ongoing access to fellowships and paid work through the Shifters 100 alumni network that corporate partners recruit from. Free to enter.

Duration: Submissions close 31 August 2026; finalists announced October, winners December 2026

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Social Shifters, a Scottish-registered charity backed by corporate partners, runs its Global Innovation Challenge as a funnel into a longer founder-development pipeline rather than a one-off prize. ELIGIBILITY: founders aged 18 to 30 at submission, leading a fully youth-led venture that is already live and producing measurable social or environmental impact, aligned to at least one Sustainable Development Goal; concept-stage ideas and student projects do not qualify. Multiple winners receive up to USD 15,000 each, plus pitch coaching, a finalist showcase, and ongoing access to fellowships and paid work through the Shifters 100 alumni network. Free coaching is available from the moment you register, so the payoff to registering early is high. Submissions close 31 August 2026 (5pm UTC), with finalists announced in October and winners in December 2026. Apply at https://www.socialshifters.co/global-innovation-challenge/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Made of Truth – Short Non-Fiction Film Fund (UK)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK (both director and producer must be UK residents)

Award: Up to £25,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Short films 5–40 minutes; up to 13 projects funded per year

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

BFI Doc Society's Made of Truth fund supports early-career UK filmmakers making non-fiction live-action or animated short films of 5–40 minutes. Up to 13 projects are funded per annual round. AMOUNT: up to £25,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: early-career directors and producers who are UK residents; teams must have at least two people (a producer and a director); directors cannot have received a commercial distribution deal for a non-fiction feature in the last five years; a reasonable proportion of early-career crew is required. Applications must meet at least three of six priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. OPENS: 18 May 2026 at 1pm UK time. DEADLINE: 1 July 2026 at 1pm UK time. APPLY via the BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Iméra: 'This Is Not Another Fellowship' – Postdoctoral Residency 2026-27 (Aix-Marseille)
item.description:
Organisation: Iméra – Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université

Location: Aix-Marseille, France (campus residency)

Award: Free accommodation in campus apartment; travel expenses covered; office in co-working space. No cash stipend specified. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months: either November 2, 2026 – January 26, 2027, or March 1, 2027 – May 28, 2027.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Iméra's 'This Is Not Another Fellowship' is a 3-month postdoctoral residency at the Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université, offered in two semester options: November 2, 2026 – January 26, 2027, or March 1, 2027 – May 28, 2027. PROVIDED: free accommodation in one of fourteen campus apartments; travel expenses covered; desk in shared open co-working space; participation in the weekly Community Building Seminar (mandatory 3-hour Thursday sessions); access to interdisciplinary scholarly networks. ELIGIBILITY: under 35 years old by May 28, 2027; currently holding a postdoctoral fellowship contract at a university; have not lived in France for more than 12 months in the prior 3 years; if requiring a visa, must demonstrate sufficient funds. Research must align with one of four Iméra programs: Arts & Sciences: Indisciplined Knowledge; Interdisciplinary Dialogues; Mediterranean; or Necessary Utopias. All disciplines welcome. DEADLINE: 3 July 2026 at 1pm (CET+2). Apply at https://www.imera.fr/en/this-is-not-another-fellowship-application-2026-27/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: National Gallery of Canada

Location: Ottawa, Canada; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada.

Award: Up to $5,000 (including expenses and/or stipend). No application fee.

Duration: One calendar year, beginning 1 September 2026.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

The National Gallery of Canada's Rodger and Joann McLennan Canadian War Art Research Fellowship encourages and supports advanced research in Canadian and Indigenous Canadian war and military art. Research must relate to any aspect of Canadian war or military art (including painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, printmaking, and other media) and must emphasise the use and investigation of the collections of the National Gallery of Canada, the Canadian War Museum, and Library and Archives Canada. The Fellowship supports original research by scholars undertaking publishing, exhibition, thesis, and dissertation work or their equivalents. AMOUNT: up to $5,000, including expenses and/or stipend. TENURE: one calendar year beginning 1 September 2026; tenable only at the National Gallery of Canada. ELIGIBILITY: open to art historians, curators, critics, conservators, graduate students, and independent or other professionals working in the visual arts, museology, and related disciplines in the humanities and social sciences; international applicants welcome. Researchers from outside Canada must ensure all visa requirements and documentation are in order before entry and the start of the Fellowship. Applications are invited from all qualified candidates, including Indigenous peoples, women, people of any sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression, racialized people, and people with disabilities. TO APPLY: submit a complete application in English or French as a single PDF by email to Amy Rose, Senior Manager, Library, Archives and Research Fellowship Program, at erefel@gallery.ca. Deadline: 26 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Musikfonds: STIP-6 AI Scholarship Programme 2027 (Germany)
item.description:
Organisation: musikfonds e.V.

Location: Germany. Open to Germany-based professional freelance musicians; no computer-science background required.

Award: EUR 30,000 lump sum per scholarship, paid in twelve monthly installments across January to December 2027. Ten scholarships available. No application fee.

Duration: Twelve months (January to December 2027).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

STIP-6 is a Musikfonds scholarship programme supporting professional musicians who work on AI-based composition and who reflect critically and societally on the role of artificial intelligence in music. Ten scholarships of EUR 30,000 each are awarded for the 2027 working year. Open to Germany-based professional freelance musicians across genres; no computer-science or technical background is required. Deadline 30 June 2026, 18:00 CET. Details and application via the Musikfonds funding programmes page (apply at musikfonds-antrag.de). NOTE: this entry was sourced from research and the funder's pages may update; confirm current figures and deadline before applying.

item.title: MAK Center: Artists and Architects in Residence 2027/2028 (MAK Schindler Scholarship, Los Angeles)
item.description:
Organisation: MAK Center for Art and Architecture, Los Angeles

Location: Los Angeles, California, United States (Mackey Apartments). Residents must be present in L.A. for the full six-month term.

Award: Six-month MAK Schindler Scholarship residency with accommodation provided at the Mackey Apartments. The open call does not state a stipend, travel or honorarium figure; confirm any additional financial support directly with the MAK Center. No application fee stated.

Duration: Six months. Residency terms run October to March or April to September; 2027/2028 cycle.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

The MAK Center for Art and Architecture awards Artists and Architects in Residence scholarships (the MAK Schindler Scholarship) to independent projects exploring the relation between art and architecture. Projects are chosen for their ability to critically examine current trends in art, architecture and society; to make a compelling connection with Los Angeles as the place of implementing or continuing the applicant's own work; and to demonstrate a conceptual and experimental approach. Emphasis is placed on the L.A. location, and residents' projects often reflect, incorporate and address specific facets of the city and its social fabric. Recipients are encouraged to take part in the MAK Center's activities and to attend meetings, workshops and project discussions with other artists and students. ELIGIBILITY: freelance artists; fine arts or architecture students at a university who have completed the '2. Studienabschnitt'; or architects or artists who have recently graduated. Teams of up to three are eligible if all members practice together as an artist group or collective of architects, are all present in L.A. for the six months, and name a contact responsible for the team. HOUSING: the Mackey Apartments are made available to scholarship holders only; family members, relatives and friends may be accommodated for a maximum of two weeks during the term, permitted in advance by Beth Stryker, Director of the MAK Center. Eligibility is excluded if the applicant has been or will be granted another scholarship in the USA immediately before or after the MAK scholarship, or if the data form is incomplete or the required portfolio is missing. Residency terms run October to March and April to September. Deadline 12 August 2026. Apply: https://www.makcenter.org/apply-mackey

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eurostars Call 11 2026 (Eureka / Eurostars-3)
item.description:
Organisation: Eureka / Eurostars-3 (Dutch participation via RVO)

Location: International consortia from at least two Eurostars countries, led by an innovative SME. Dutch funding administered by RVO.

Award: Co-funds collaborative international R&D; national funding varies by country. For the Dutch project share: up to EUR 500,000 per project at 50 percent reimbursement of eligible R&D costs. No application fee.

Duration: Projects up to 36 months.

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

Eurostars (part of the Eureka network, the Eurostars-3 programme co-funded by Horizon Europe) supports collaborative international R&D projects led by innovative SMEs. Each country funds its own participants, so amounts vary; for the Dutch share, RVO funds up to EUR 500,000 per project at a 50 percent reimbursement rate on eligible R&D costs. Projects require an international consortium of at least two independent organisations from at least two Eurostars countries, led by an innovative SME, with the SME budget at least 50 percent of the total and no single participant exceeding 70 percent. Projects must run no longer than 36 months and be civil (non-defence) in purpose. Call 11 opens 9 July 2026 and closes 10 September 2026, 14:00 CEST. No application fee. Submit via the Eureka project platform (myeurekaproject.org); Dutch national information at RVO.

Open call details

item.title: MIT R&D Collaboration Projects 2026 (MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren)
item.description:
Organisation: Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (RVO)

Location: Netherlands. SME consortia (minimum two collaborating SMEs).

Award: Grant covering 35 percent of eligible costs. Small projects EUR 50,000-200,000; large projects EUR 200,000-350,000 per project (EUR 25,000-100,000 or EUR 25,000-175,000 per participant). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; ranked tender (not first-come).

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The MIT R&D Collaboration Projects scheme (MIT R&D-samenwerkingsprojecten, part of MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren) funds joint R&D projects by Dutch SMEs within the national Top Sectors and Mission-driven Innovation policy. The grant covers 35 percent of eligible costs: small projects of EUR 50,000-200,000 and large projects of EUR 200,000-350,000 (with per-participant ranges of EUR 25,000-100,000 for small and EUR 25,000-175,000 for large). Eligible applicants are consortia of at least two collaborating SMEs, with no single participant carrying more than 70 percent of costs. The national RVO round runs 9 June to 15 September 2026 (closes 17:00) and is a ranked tender, not first-come-first-served; some provinces run separate regional MIT windows. No application fee. Apply via RVO.

item.title: Expo Bart: OPEN HALL 2026 (Free Project Space, Technique & Support)
item.description:
Organisation: Expo Bart

Location: Expo Bart, NYMA, Waalplein 6, Nijmegen, Netherlands

Award: No fee and no rent, but also no artist fee or other compensation (geen vergoeding). Provides free use of a 100 m2 fully blackout-able project space, limited technique (2 short-throw beamers, a PA set with mixer and microphones, and light spots) and helping hands, plus promotion of your event via Expo Bart's channels.

Duration: Programme runs 13 November to 20 December 2026; book one or more days (open Thursday to Sunday, 12:00-17:00; other times negotiable)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Do you have an idea you have always wanted to carry out but can never find the space for? Expo Bart in Nijmegen invites you to apply for OPEN HALL. From 13 November to 20 December 2026, Expo Bart makes its project space, technique and helping hands available to help realise your idea, whether that is a week-long exhibition, a one-day workshop, a film shoot, a knitting club, a concert series, a film night, a performance, a dinner, a combination of all of these, or something completely different. PRACTICAL: you can sign up for one or more days between 13 November and 20 December 2026; Expo Bart is open Thursday to Sunday from 12:00 to 17:00 (organising something outside opening hours can be discussed together); the 100 m2 project space is fully blackout-able; limited technique is available, namely two short-throw beamers, a PA set (with mixer and microphones) and light spots; and your event is of course shared via Expo Bart's channels. There is no compensation and no rent is charged. APPLY by sending your idea (max 1 A4, including photos) to info@expobart.nl with the subject line 'OPEN HALL', stating your plan/idea, your preferred day(s), and what you need in terms of production. Deadline: 12 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Fluxus Experimental Film & Media Arts Festival 2026: Call for Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Hamilton Artists Inc., in partnership with McMaster University School of the Arts and Factory Media Centre

Location: Hamilton, Ontario, Canada; submitting artists must be currently based and/or working in Canada.

Award: Free to submit (NO entry fee). IMAA-scale artist remuneration (a screening/exhibition fee following the Independent Media Arts Alliance fee schedule) is paid to successful applicants within 30 days of the screenings.

Duration: Two screenings, 18 and 19 September 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Fluxus is a contemporary short film and media arts festival hosted in partnership between Factory Media Centre, Hamilton Artists Inc and McMaster University School of the Arts. Its mission is to provide an accessible forum for the exhibition of creative, experimental, moving-image art forms, and to develop connections between Canadian media artists, arts institutions and the public. Fluxus showcases experimental and boundary-pushing media works from artists of all levels working in Canada; submissions are free and unrestricted by theme, open to a spectrum of artforms including but not limited to animation, documentary, video art, collage film, music video and experimental film. The festival takes place over two screenings at Hamilton Artists Inc on Friday 18 and Saturday 19 September 2026 (the Friday screening is dedicated to emerging media artists and filmmakers); all screenings are free to attend. REMUNERATION: there is no submission fee, and IMAA-scale artist remuneration is paid to successful applicants within 30 days of the screenings. GUIDELINES: artists must be currently based and/or working in Canada; work must be short format (15 minutes or less) and no more than 5 years old (2021 or later); submissions are digital Vimeo or YouTube links (private/password-protected accepted if the password is provided); solo artists, groups, collectives and collaborative works are welcome, with a limit of one submission per artist/group/collective; films must be independent projects over which the artist retains full creative and artistic control. AI POLICY: Fluxus discourages the use of generative AI in submitted works; if AI factors into the creative process, its use must be declared and described (see Factory Media Centre's Policy on the Use of Generative AI). APPLY via the online submission form with an artist bio (75 words max), artist CV (PDF, 3 pages max), a brief description of the work (100 words max), entry information, the video link, and two image stills. Notification is expected in August; due to the high quantity of submissions, only selected artists will be contacted. Fluxus is committed to equity and strongly encourages applications from Black and Indigenous artists, persons with disabilities, ethnic or racial minorities, immigrants and refugees, Francophones, LGBTQI+ persons, women, and persons of all socio-economic backgrounds. Deadline: Wednesday 15 July 2026 at 11:59pm EST. Questions: programming@theinc.ca.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Frame Fellowship 2.0 (Residential Accelerator for AI Safety Content Creators) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Frame Fellowship

Location: San Francisco, USA; residential (in person).

Award: USD 10,000 stipend for the 10 weeks, plus accommodation, meals and flights covered. Access to production-ready studios in San Francisco, a mentor and creator network, and connections to funding, sponsorship and partnership opportunities at organizations such as FLI, CAIS and Seismic. Top performers may be eligible for additional grant opportunities beyond the program. No application fee.

Duration: 10-week residential program

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

Frame Fellowship is a 10-week residential accelerator in San Francisco for content creators educating the public on AI safety. Frame's premise is that content creators can solve a long-standing bottleneck of expanding reach and trust beyond the AI safety and effective altruism (EA) bubble; creators today hack attention, empower mass action and shape culture. Past Frame Fellows include award-winning filmmakers, journalists from leading newsrooms and ex-activists who transitioned to educating, empowering and preparing their communities for the impacts of AGI. Frame helps amplify their impact to millions of people, connects them to funding and sponsorship opportunities at organizations such as FLI, CAIS and Seismic, and helps establish their path as a leading content creator. This cohort runs 10 weeks with a stronger mentor and creator network, access to production-ready studios in San Francisco, housing, food and a stipend, plus connections to funding and partnership opportunities after the program. TWO TRACKS: (1) Independent Track, build your own channel and become a defining voice on a topic; (2) Amplifier Track, embed with an AI org/company and grow their reach. SUPPORT: Frame covers accommodation, meals, flights and a USD 10,000 stipend for the 10 weeks so fellows can go all in; top performers may be eligible for additional grant opportunities beyond the program. Early deadline: 10 July 2026. Apply at https://framefellowship.com/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FUTURES Photography: 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: FUTURES Photography

Location: Futures Hub, Amsterdam, Netherlands; ISSP, Riga, Latvia; Capa Center, Budapest, Hungary

Award: EUR 1,000 stipend; accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 1 month; location-specific dates in 2027 (Amsterdam: Jan, Feb, Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec; Riga: May; Budapest: Apr)

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

ELIGIBILITY: Open exclusively to current FUTURES artists (existing members of the FUTURES Photography platform); the application is free of charge. CONCEPT: The residency supports emerging photographers with time, space, and resources to develop new work, with a strong emphasis on research and artistic process. Artists are invited to explore the intersection of Nature, Environment, and Technology - projects should challenge perceptions of human and ecological relationships, technological advancement, and environmental responsibility. An interdisciplinary approach is encouraged, and each residency culminates in a public event where artists share their work and engage with diverse audiences. LOCATIONS: Three partner venues host residencies at different times in 2027. (1) Futures Hub, Amsterdam (global FUTURES HQ): a 60 sqm ground-floor studio with heating, electricity, and private entrance; live-work space with double folding bed, private bathroom, washing machine, and small kitchen; sliding door separates work and living areas; access to a shared atrium for large-scale works, performances, and presentations; communal rooftop terrace; bed linens and towels provided; available January, February, April, May, June, September, October, November, December 2027. (2) ISSP, Riga: private bedroom with working space in a shared city-centre apartment with two other artists; spacious shared living room and fully equipped kitchen; access to ISSP office facilities including printer, scanner, ISSP photography library, and studio lights; available May 2027. (3) Capa Center, Budapest: fully furnished private room in a communal apartment at Art Quarter Budapest; 400 sqm shared studio in a postindustrial area; services to integrate residents into the local Budapest art scene and establish professional connections; option to organise a presentation at Capa Centre in downtown Budapest; access to Capa Centre office facilities (printer, scanner); available April 2027. PRESENCE: minimum 90% presence required throughout the residency period. ENGAGEMENT: residents contribute to a Visual Diary (one Instagram carousel and two Stories, in collaboration with FUTURES), a short Video Portrait documenting their process, a public event such as an open studio or workshop (open studios on the last Wednesday of the month), and a written interview with a local writer published on the FUTURES website. FINANCIAL SUPPORT: EUR 1,000 stipend to cover living expenses, materials, and travel; accommodation is provided at all locations. Residents are responsible for arranging travel documents, health and travel insurance, liability insurance, and any visa requirements (support provided where needed). APPLY: via the FUTURES website; application is free and open only to FUTURES artists. TIMELINE: deadline 22 June 2026 at 23:59 Amsterdam time; results announced July 2026. Contact info@futures-photography.com for questions.

Open call details

item.title: The Cooper Prize 2026 (16th Street Foundation and Company 16, Melbourne)
item.description:
Organisation: 16th Street Foundation and Company 16

Location: Melbourne, Australia (international eligibility)

Award: AUD 20,000 prize to winner, plus consideration for a full commission and production of the winning work. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award); winning work may proceed to full commission and production.

Deadline: 19 Jun 2026

The Cooper Prize 2026 is an international playwriting prize administered by 16th Street Foundation and Company 16 in Melbourne, Australia. AUD 20,000 is awarded to the winning playwright, plus consideration for a full commission and professional production. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. Open internationally to published and unpublished playwrights at any career stage with no geographic restrictions. Submissions must respond to thematic prompts drawn from Victor Frankl's 'Man's Search for Meaning' and Dostoevsky's 'The Brothers Karamazov', exploring themes including doubt versus faith, the meaning of suffering, and the power of truth. Application requires: (1) a one-page document covering motivation for writing, issues to explore, philosophical perspective or worldview, and a synopsis or treatment; (2) ten pages of the play manuscript. Judging panel led by Iain Sinclair and Harry Haynes. Deadline: 19 June 2026. Apply via JotForm at https://form.jotform.com/260408855140858.

Open call details

The BFI Doc Society Expanded Screen Fund supports ambitious screen-based works of non-fiction intended to reach audiences beyond cinemas, including VR, AR, MR, and 360-degree experiences. ELIGIBILITY: producers with a demonstrated track record in delivering immersive work; UK-based; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk-taking, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. item.title: Project Liberty: Co-Lab Fund Member Collaboration Grants (Round 1, 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Project Liberty (Alliance SteerCo); pooled fund supported by Future of Life Institute, Omidyar Network, Ergo Impact, and Project Liberty

Location: Members of the Project Liberty coalition (Alliance). Projects led by a Coalition member organization; geography otherwise unrestricted.

Award: Grants cap at USD 250,000 per project; most first-round awards fall between USD 25,000 and USD 100,000. Pooled fund supported by Future of Life Institute, Omidyar Network, Ergo Impact, and Project Liberty. No application fee.

Duration: Project length varies. Funding rounds run monthly until December 2026, or until the funds are disbursed; decisions are expected within roughly three weeks of each deadline.

Deadline: 21 Jun 2026

MEMBERS ONLY: this is a member-led collaboration fund, not an open public call. Projects must be led by a Project Liberty Coalition (Alliance) member organization. The Co-Lab Fund, launched by the coalition's Steering Committee (SteerCo), supports projects that Coalition members undertake together, building on the trust and relationships that already exist within the community so that funding decisions can rest on the strength of the idea, the people behind it, and their track records rather than extensive diligence. In exchange for a streamlined process, grants stay modest and funded teams share their progress with the broader Coalition as projects develop, creating accountability and letting the community learn from what emerges. Two kinds of projects qualify: (1) work that advances the Coalition's active workstreams (listed in the application), and (2) investment in the Coalition's connective infrastructure, such as shared services and resources that strengthen collective capacity. Eligibility: projects must be led by a Coalition member organization, produce value for more than one Coalition organization, begin substantive work within a few weeks of approval, and fall within the active workstreams; full eligibility details are in the Overview and FAQ document. How it works: monthly rounds are planned until December 2026 or until the funds are disbursed; the application is designed to take an hour or less once the project's shape is clear, and decisions are expected within about three weeks of each deadline. This is the first cycle and the Fund is being built as it goes, so the process is expected to improve each round. Round 1 closes Sunday, 21 June 2026; further rounds follow, so there is no need to force an idea into this cycle. Apply via the Fillout application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TechCongress: Congressional Innovation Fellowship 2027 (Senior and Early-Career)
item.description:
Organisation: TechCongress

Location: Washington, DC, United States. Full-time, in-person; remote participation is not possible. Nonpartisan: fellows serve in both Democratic and Republican offices.

Award: Senior (mid-career) Fellows: USD 100,600/year stipend. Early-career Fellows: USD 78,000 annual-equivalent stipend (USD 6,500/month). Plus health insurance supplement up to USD 425/month, up to USD 2,000 relocation to DC, up to USD 2,000 first-month short-term housing reimbursement, up to USD 2,000 official travel, and a USD 500 professional-attire allowance. No application fee.

Duration: Senior Fellows serve January to December 2027 (optional one-month extension). Early-career Fellows serve January to October 2027.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

TechCongress places technologists in the US Congress to build technology capacity among Members and Congressional staff, where fewer than 3% of legislative staff have a technical background. For the January 2027 cohort it is recruiting up to twenty fellows (10 for Democratic offices, 10 for Republican offices) across two tiers: the Senior Congressional Innovation Fellowship (mid-career, at least eight years of work or postgraduate study) and the Congressional Innovation Fellowship (early-career, two to six years of experience, including a recently finished or projected-by-January-2027 graduate technical degree such as a Masters or PhD; applicants with seven years qualify for an intermediate tier). Fellows choose a placement with a Member of Congress or a Congressional Committee, report to a senior staffer (such as a Chief of Staff or Staff Director), and do work similar to other Congressional staff: researching tech policy (cyber and election security, data and biometric privacy, AI policy, autonomous vehicles, health IT, encryption, disinformation), educating Members and staff, writing legislation, preparing Committee hearings, markups and investigations, and building coalitions. No prior government experience is required; the fellowship is first and foremost an educational experience. This year TechCongress also seeks at least two fellows from non-traditional verticals: climate (grid modernization, clean energy, NEPA reform), government innovation (govtech and digital benefits delivery, civil service reform), and science, research and innovation (NSF, NIH, DoE, FDA reform, clinical trials). Technical degrees or experience in engineering, development, journalism, scientific, legal or government roles are encouraged. TechCongress is a nonpartisan nonprofit incubated at the Open Technology Institute at New America; since 2016 it has sent 140 fellows to Congress. Equal opportunity employer. Application deadline: 26 June 2026, 5:59am (applicant's local timezone). Contact: aleena@techcongress.io. Apply via the official application form.

Open call details

item.title: APWLD: Breaking Out of Marginalisation (BOOM) Feminist Participatory Action Research, Feminist Food Sovereignty 2026-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development (APWLD)

Location: Asia and the Pacific; open to national and/or grassroots women-led organisations in the region (Central Asia, East Asia and the Pacific sub-regions especially encouraged).

Award: Sub-grant of up to USD 14,000 per selected partner organisation to support the employment of a young woman researcher and cover direct research, implementation, advocacy and capacity-building costs (not institutional sustainability or maintenance). Also includes capacity-building workshops, regional training, advocacy and networking opportunities, and ongoing accompaniment. No application fee.

Duration: 16-month FPAR implementation (2026-2028 cycle)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

APWLD invites grassroots women's organisations and movements in Asia and the Pacific to take part in a Feminist Participatory Action Research (FPAR) cycle on Feminist Food Sovereignty, supporting women-led and community-driven research, collective action and advocacy that reclaim community control over food systems, natural resources and knowledge while challenging corporate control and structural inequalities in food and agriculture. Six national grassroots women-led organisations will be selected to work with their communities to conduct FPAR, generate community-led evidence and develop collective advocacy from 2026 to 2028. THEMATIC STREAMS (partners may focus on one or more): community-led sustainable food systems (women-led agroecology, ecological farming, biodiversity restoration, farmer-to-farmer learning); seed sovereignty and Indigenous knowledge systems (women as seed keepers, community seed banks, resistance to corporate seed regimes); land, water and territorial rights (struggles against land and water grabbing, community governance of commons); and access to food justice and systemic transformation (legal, policy and advocacy strategies, cooperatives, community markets, alternative food economies). APWLD prioritises initiatives that strengthen Indigenous knowledge, collective governance, ecological sustainability, feminist transformation and women's leadership. SUPPORT: a sub-grant of up to USD 14,000 per partner over 16 months. Each selected partner identifies two women, a mentor and a young woman researcher below 35, at least one from the community or constituency where the organisation has ongoing food sovereignty work (grassroots, rural or Indigenous women, small-scale food producers, peasants, fisherfolk, or women advancing women-led food sovereignty alternatives). ELIGIBILITY: non-governmental, non-profit, women-led and/or grassroots organisations with experience working with grassroots women, ongoing food-sovereignty work, capacity to conduct participatory feminist community-led research, and ability to work in English (the main working language for training and reporting); a recommendation/reference letter from at least one women's or grassroots organisation is required. APPLY via the online Google Form or by emailing the completed application form to eloisa@apwld.org and tasmiah@apwld.org. Deadline: Sunday 5 July 2026. NOTE: this is an organisation-only sub-grant in the food sovereignty / agroecology and gender-justice space rather than an individual arts/AI opportunity.

Open call details

item.title: Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency 2026: Writing About Natural Places, Stewards, and the Spaces We Care For
item.description:
Organisation: Upstream

Location: Remote residency in Minnesota, USA; applicants must be Minnesota residents living in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties).

Award: USD 3,000 artist stipend, plus mileage reimbursement up to USD 500, a Minnesota State Parks Pass, mentorship and creative support, and the opportunity to publish and share work statewide. No application fee.

Duration: 9-month remote residency

Deadline: 06 Jul 2026

The Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency invites creative writers, poets, storytellers and grassroots journalists to spend intentional time over nine months exploring Minnesota's parks, waterways, forests and natural places, and the people who care for them. The residency seeks writers who care about storytelling rooted in place, environment, community, stewardship and connection. SELECTED MINNESOTA ARTISTS RECEIVE: a USD 3,000 artist stipend; mileage reimbursement up to USD 500; a Minnesota State Parks Pass; mentorship and creative support; and the opportunity to publish and share their work statewide. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be a Minnesota resident, 18 years or older, with a residence in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range, Minnesota (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties). Applications open 8 June 2026; deadline 6 July 2026 by 11:59pm. Apply via the online JotForm.

Open call details

item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Genius Loci Weimar: Video Mapping Competition 2026 (Democracy and Society)
item.description:
Organisation: Genius Loci Weimar

Location: Weimar, Germany. Open to international artists; winning works are projected onto historic facades during the festival.

Award: EUR 7,000 production budget per winning facade (one winner selected per building) plus a EUR 3,000 festival prize for one selected work. No application fee.

Duration: Festival 11-13 September 2026; deliverable is a roughly 5-minute projection-mapping work for a facade.

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Genius Loci Weimar is an annual projection-mapping (video mapping) festival that invites artists to design audiovisual works for the facades of historic Weimar buildings. The 2026 competition theme is Democracy and Society. Entry is at concept stage: submissions are conceptual proposals, and professional video-mapping expertise is not required, as the organisers provide technical support to realise winning concepts. One winner is selected per facade and receives a EUR 7,000 production budget; one selected work additionally receives a EUR 3,000 festival prize. Open to international artists working as individuals or in teams. Winners are announced around 6 July 2026 and the festival runs 11-13 September 2026. Application deadline: 14 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Register and submit via the official competition page.

Open call details

item.title: Music WorX: Music-Tech Incubator 2026 (Hamburg)
item.description:
Organisation: Hamburg Kreativ Gesellschaft (Kreativ Gesellschaft)

Location: Hamburg, Germany. Open to European founders and founding teams; Hamburg-anchored program with a European mentor network.

Award: EUR 3,200 development budget per team plus a EUR 1,500 per month living allowance across the 3-month programme. End-of-programme prizes: EUR 1,000 audience award and EUR 2,500 jury award. No equity is taken. No application fee.

Duration: 3-month programme, roughly early September to early December 2026.

Deadline: 17 Jun 2026

Music WorX is the music-technology incubator run by Hamburg Kreativ Gesellschaft for early-stage teams building products at the intersection of music and technology (creation tools, interactive media, streaming and monetisation, AI for music, instruments and audio gear). Selected teams receive a EUR 3,200 development budget for support services plus a EUR 1,500 per month living allowance over the 3-month programme, mentoring from a European music-tech network, and the chance to win a EUR 1,000 audience prize and a EUR 2,500 jury prize at demo day. The incubator takes no equity. Open to European founders and founding teams (teams of at least two recommended, solo founders welcome); no formal company formation is required to apply. The programme runs roughly September to December 2026. Application deadline: 17 June 2026. Apply via the Airtable form linked from the official programme page.

Open call details
